matches.x: CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
BILDAD THE SHUHITE... 1. Not much know about him 2. May have been a descendent of Shuah, son of Abraham and Keturah, who lived in the "east" (Gen 25:2,6)
CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
BILDAD THE SHUHITE... 1. Not much know about him 2. May have been a descendent of Shuah, son of Abraham and Keturah, who lived in the "east" (Gen 25:2,6)
BILDAD'S FINAL RESPONSE (25:1-6) 1. He proclaims the greatness of God (25:1-3) a. Dominion and fear belong to Him, He makes peace in His high places b. His armies are innumerable
BILDAD'S FINAL RESPONSE (25:1-6) 1. He proclaims the greatness of God (25:1-3) a. Dominion and fear belong to Him, He makes peace in His high places b. His armies are innumerable
» And when he smileth at thee he is probing thee®®. τ ruthless one maketh peace, While plotting against the life of many’.

matches.lb: Jewish Thought: Duties of the Heart, Seventh Treatise on Repentance, Introduction 6
matches.x: Among them, what the verse says: "for the imagination of man's heart is evil from his youth" (Bereishis 8:21), and "the L-ord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually" (Bereishis 6:5), and "a man is born as a wild donkey's colt" (Iyov 11:12), and "Behold, the moon has no brightness"(Iyov 25:5) and "how…
